content:
Coordinate worker delegation for this round.
Target agents: POOL-0..POOL-3.
Available shapes: 23 shapes available (23 unprocessed).
Batch target: 8 shapes.

OPTIMIZED SHAPE DISTRIBUTION (batch-size=2, 4 workers, 8 total shapes):
POOL-0: shapes [0..2) - InstallationShape (class: Installation) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/Installation/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}], EmissionPointShape (class: EmissionPoint) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/emissiepunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}]
POOL-1: shapes [2..4) - WithdrawalPointShape (class: WithdrawalPoint) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/onttrekkingspunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}], MeasurementPointShape (class: MeasurementPoint) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/meetpunt/{uuid}/{issued}/{created}]
POOL-2: shapes [4..6) - ProcessShape (class: Process) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/proces/{localId}/{issued}/{created}], MeasurementInstrumentShape (class: MeasurementInstrument)
POOL-3: shapes [6..8) - FilterShape (class: Filter), OperationLocationShape (class: OperationLocation) [IRI template: https://example.org/id/exploitatielocatie/{geoId}/{issued}/{created}]

Delegate immediately using these assignments.
NOTE: a shape's name is the validation rule's name, not necessarily a class name - never assume it is one. Tell each worker to type instances with the class shown in parentheses after each shape above, not with the shape's own name.
NOTE: where a class above is shown with '[IRI template: ...]', that literal RFC 6570 template was resolved directly from the ontology's hydra:search declaration for that class - copy it verbatim into the worker's assignment and instruct them to substitute real values for each {variable}. Do not paraphrase or shorten it, and do not invent a different URI scheme for that class. A class with no template shown has no hydra:search declaration; for those, restate the ontology's default prefix and a consistent local-name convention instead.

First pass: delegate the next batch and keep instructions concise.

Use SHACL context as source of truth for constraints.

Delegate immediately:
- Call `delegate_tasks` for each worker that should run this round.
- Keep each assignment short and concrete.
- Include target shape(s), target class, and whether to CREATE new instances or FIX existing ones.
- Include unresolved focus node/property details when available from Validation Report.
- Require worker tool call: worker_progress(status, target_shape, target_class, changed_triples_count, validation_result, remaining_issues).
- In every assignment, restate the exact IRI pattern new instances must use. First check whether the
  Ontology context declares a `hydra:search [ a hydra:IriTemplate ; hydra:template "..."^^hydra:Rfc6570Template ;
  hydra:mapping [...] ]` for the assigned target class - if so, paste that literal template string
  into the assignment and tell the worker to substitute real values for each `{variable}` (per its
  hydra:mapping) rather than invent their own scheme. Only when a class has no hydra:search template,
  fall back to restating: the ontology's default prefix (the one shown as `@prefix : <...>` or
  `@prefix ontologynamehere: <...>` in the Ontology/SHACL context - pick ONE and tell every worker to
  use that SAME one, not a mix), followed by the exact class local name with a logical and consistent
  URI. Workers across different rounds/pools must not invent their own casing or numbering
  convention for the same class. Note: a resolved hydra template contains `/` in its path, so it must
  be written as a full `<...>` IRI, never as a `prefix:LocalName` prefixed name (which cannot contain
  an unescaped `/`). Show the worker the correct Turtle syntax directly in the assignment, using
  the assigned class's OWN resolved template and a real placeholder value, in this shape:
    CORRECT:   <resolved-template-with-real-values> a :TargetClass ; ...
    WRONG:     :TargetClass/some-id a :TargetClass ; ...   (slash in a prefixed name - parse error)
  Always substitute the assignment's actual template/class/id - never invent or reuse a fixed
  example class/domain of your own across assignments.
- In every assignment, give a short concrete example: one representative instance (its IRI built
  from the pattern above, plus its 2-3 most important property values) so workers across pools stay
  consistent instead of each inventing their own shape/values for the same class. Ground it in a
  brief scenario summary consistent with the overall user-input request.
- Remind workers that predicates/classes must come only from the ontology/SHACL context - never
  invented - and that each triplestore_add call is validated as a standalone Turtle document.

Rules:
- Do not produce long analysis text; prioritize immediate tool calls.
- Skip workers with no actionable assignment.
- Always instruct workers to create instances when assigned target class has no instances.
- Keep workers scoped to assigned shapes/classes only.
- STOP as soon as you have called `delegate_tasks` for every worker that should run this round.
  Workers only run after this response ends - none of them have run yet, so calling
  context_reader/context_read again to "check for progress" or calling delegate_tasks a second
  time for a worker you already assigned will only ever see the same unchanged state. That is
  expected, not a sign anything is stuck. Worker results become visible next round.
context_reader({"action":"list"})

{"length":613,"name":"example file","type":"text/plain","content":"MyGlassFactory is a glass factory that produces glass. They have a factory (Operation) that deploys
multiple systems. This Operation is located on a location (platform).

The factory implements a process that is subdivided into multiple steps that describe the process of producing glass. A process
can be subdivided into other processes.

An emission point is a point where emissions are released into the environment. A proces may be implemented by an
emission point (or other system).

Multiple versions of the factory (operation) exist. Versions may have an overlap in systems, processes but also differences."}
Now I have all the information needed. Let me delegate to all 4 workers with precise shape/class assignments, IRI templates from hydra:search declarations, and concrete examples.

|--------|--------|---------|------------|
| POOL-0 | InstallationShape, EmissionPointShape | :Installation, :EmissionPoint | hydra:search templates |
| POOL-1 | WithdrawalPointShape, MeasurementPointShape | :WithdrawalPoint, :MeasurementPoint | hydra:search templates |

| POOL-3 | FilterShape, OperationLocationShape | :Filter, :OperationLocation | Filter: default prefix; OL: hydra:search |

Each worker has:
- Exact IRI patterns (RFC 6570 templates substituted with placeholder values)
- Mandatory SHACL constraints (minCount=1 properties)

- Instructions to use only ontology/SHACL predicates
- Required `worker_progress` reporting format

Workers will now execute. Progress reports will be available in the next round via context changes.
